历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plan
历年位次与招生计划.text: From 2021 to 2025, the minimum admission rankings for the Physics Category (including Science Category from 2021-2023) in Heilongjiang Province were approximately 6901, 6421, 5400, 4924, and 5669 respectively. From 2021 to 2024, there was a continuous forward trend (decreasing ranking numbers), but in 2025, there was a significant backward shift (ranking number increased to 5669), moving back about 745 places compared to 2024, with large fluctuations. The 5-year average is about 5863, and the 2025 baseline ranking is about 5669. The enrollment plan for this university in the Physics Category (Science) in Heilongjiang Province: 539 in 2021, 541 in 2022, 533 in 2023, 513 in 2024, 518 in 2025, and 500 in 2026 (subject to the enrollment plan document). The 2026 plan is 18 fewer than 2025, a reduction of about 3.5%, and the tightening of enrollment quotas has a certain impact on the competitive landscape.

学校层次与声誉.title: University Level and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: Harbin Engineering University is a 211 Project and national Double First-Class construction university. Its ranking in the Shanghai Ranking's Best Chinese Universities has been steadily rising: 50th in 2021, 53rd in 2022, 49th in 2023, 45th in 2024, 43rd in 2025, and 43rd in 2026, firmly in the national top 50, with steadily improving comprehensive strength and recognition. The university's overall popularity ranking on Douyin (TikTok) is 27th (relatively hot), and it was ranked 6th (very hot) in 2023. Well-known education planners like Zhang Xuefeng have repeatedly recommended its top majors such as Underwater Acoustics Engineering and Nuclear Engineering, with high exposure on short video platforms, reflecting increasing attention from candidates. In recent years, the reputation evaluation has been stable, with no significant negative public opinion or records of admission gaps. Control Science and Engineering was rated A in the fifth round of discipline evaluation. Naval Architecture and Ocean Engineering, Nuclear Science and Technology, and Underwater Acoustics Engineering are the university's traditional flagship fields, highly corresponding to enrollment majors such as Automation, Marine Engineering, Nuclear Engineering and Nuclear Technology, and Underwater Acoustics Engineering, which strongly attract candidates in related directions.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Region
专业热度与地域.text: Majors such as Computer Science, Electronic Information/Communication, and Electrical Engineering and Automation continue to be highly recommended and popular in society, highly matching the university's enrollment categories like Automation, Electronic Information, and Computer Science, boosting application enthusiasm. The total number of candidates registered for the 2026 Heilongjiang Gaokao is about 167,000, slightly lower than 2025, with an estimated 114,000 Physics Category candidates, a small reduction in the competition base. In 2025, the Physics Category undergraduate batch line was 360 points, and the special type control line was 472 points, basically unchanged from 2024, with stable exam difficulty. Harbin Engineering University is located in Harbin, Heilongjiang Province, and is the only 211 engineering university in the province. It has a large enrollment scale in the province (about 500 in Physics Category), with strong willingness from local candidates to apply, and regional preference factors provide some support for rankings.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Considering factors such as the backward shift in 2025 rankings (5669), the 2026 enrollment plan reduction of about 3.5% (slightly easing competition), the continuous rise in the university's Shanghai Ranking (43rd), relatively high Douyin popularity and high social attention to flagship majors, and a slight decrease in the total number of candidates in the province, and taking into account the intensified competition due to the university's level improvement and increased popularity, the predicted minimum admission ranking for 2026 is about 5521, moving forward about 148 places compared to 2025. The ranking range is 5021–6021, with a half-width of 500 places.

major_tier_recommendation_en.overall_advice: Predicted ranking is about 5521, with a range of 5021–6021. It is recommended that candidates with rankings within 5000 focus on sprinting for Tier 1 Xinghai Leading/Qian Xuesen Class and other flagship major directions; candidates with rankings between 5000–5600 focus on Tier 2 majors such as Underwater Acoustics Engineering, Marine Engineering, and Nuclear Engineering as primary targets, and also apply for Tier 3 majors like Automation and Electronic Information as stable choices; candidates with rankings between 5600–6000 mainly target Tier 3 majors, and may try for some Tier 2 majors. The university's major category enrollment policy allows students to choose any major within the category after enrollment, so when applying, prioritize categories that include desired directions.

历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plan
历年位次与招生计划.text: From 2021 to 2025, the minimum admission rankings for the History Category (including Liberal Arts from 2021 to 2023) in Heilongjiang Province were approximately 1866, 1770, 1883, 2101, and 2045, respectively. The overall trend shows fluctuations, with a slight forward shift from 2021 to 2022, a notable backward shift from 2023 to 2024, and a small forward shift of about 56 places in 2025. The 5-year average is approximately 1933, with the 2025 baseline ranking around 2045. The enrollment plan for the History Category (Liberal Arts) at this university in Heilongjiang Province: 58 in 2021, 60 in 2022, 53 in 2023, 78 in 2024, 87 in 2025, and 108 in 2026 (subject to the enrollment plan document). The 2026 plan increases by 21 students compared to 2025, an expansion of about 24.1%, significantly increasing enrollment slots and greatly impacting the competitive landscape.

学校层次与声誉.title: University Level and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: Harbin Engineering University is a 211 Project and national Double First-Class university, with its Soft Science ranking continuously rising to 43rd place. However, as a science and engineering institution, its overall appeal to liberal arts students is relatively limited. Its Douyin popularity ranks 27th overall (relatively hot), but content mainly focuses on science and engineering majors, with limited exposure to liberal arts. In recent years, its reputation has been stable with no significant negative public opinion. The university's liberal arts-related disciplines did not enter the A category in the fifth round of discipline evaluations. Majors such as Law, Ideological and Political Education, and Sociology have average disciplinary foundations, but they still possess certain competitiveness due to the 211 platform.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Region
专业热度与地域.text: Law has high social popularity (relatively hot), while majors like Ideological and Political Education and Sociology have average popularity. In 2026, the estimated number of History Category examinees in Heilongjiang Province is about 53,000, a slight decrease from 54,700 in 2025, indicating a small reduction in the competitive base. The provincial control line for the History Category undergraduate batch in 2025 is 405 points, with a special type control line of 480 points, roughly unchanged from 2024. As the only 211 science and engineering university in the province, its liberal arts enrollment scale is small but continuously expanding. Local candidates' willingness to apply is stable, and regional preferences provide some support for rankings.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Considering the 2025 ranking (2045), the significant expansion of the 2026 enrollment plan by about 24.1% (intensified competition, ranking backward shift), the improved university level but limited appeal to liberal arts, and the slight decrease in the total number of History Category examinees in the province, and factoring in the large expansion but slightly reduced candidate base, the predicted minimum admission ranking for 2026 is approximately 2361, a backward shift of about 316 places from 2025. The ranking range is 2007–2715.

The university's History Category enrollment in 2026 has significantly expanded to 108 students, increasing admission opportunities.
